examples.text: وَلَا تَسُبُّوا الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ فَيَسُبُّوا اللَّهَ عَدْوًا بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ
examples.english: revile not those unto whom they pray beside God lest they revile God in enmity through ignorance

body.text: One who reviles, vilifies, upbraids, reproaches,
&c.; (A'Obeyd, S, M, Msb, * K;) as also ↓
(M, K.) A poet, (S,) namely, 'Abd-Er-Rahmán Ibn-Hassán, satirizing Miskeen Ed-Dárimee, (TA,) says,

laA tasub~an~aniY falasota bisib~iY
                      
                     
                        A_in~a sib~iY mina Alr~ijaAli Alkariymu
Thou shalt by no means revile me; for thou art not my mutual reviler: verily he, of men, who is my mutual reviler is the generous
(S, M, Mgh, Msb, K.) El-Mukhabbal Es-Saadee says, (S, M, Mgh,) using it in this sense, (M, Mgh,)

waA^a$ohadu mino EawofK HuluwlFA kaviyrapF
                      
                     
                        yaHuj~uwna sib~a Alz~iboriqaAni AlmuzaEofaraA
And I witness many persons of 'Owf, alighting during their journeys, going repeatedly to and fro to see Ez-Zibrikán's turban dyed with saffron
]: (S, M, Mgh:) for it was a custom of the chiefs of the Arabs to dye their turbans with saffron: or, as some say, the meaning is his
; [but this is correctly, or more commonly, termed
;] and Ktr asserts that he was suspected: (M:) he says that Ez-Zibrikán used to tinge his
yellow; but this is a strange saying. (TA in art.
(Aboo-'Omar, TA:) or so the latter word: (M:) or this signifies
a linen stuff that is brought from the region of the Nile, of a kind commonly known among the merchants by
some of which are made in Misr, and their length is eight by six
cubits being meant because the ns. of number here are fem., and
is fem.]: (Sh, TA:) or this same word signifies
an oblong piece of cloth of any kind,

; (S, K, TA;) and of the latter,
